A01.title: Direct Object Reference Challenge
A01.description:
This challenge demonstrates a classic Broken Access Control vulnerability through Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR).

Scenario: You are testing a user profile system that allows users to view their account information by providing a user ID parameter. The application fails to properly verify that the user requesting the information is authorized to access it.

Your Mission: Exploit the broken access control to access another user's profile information and find the hidden flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand how direct object reference vulnerabilities work
  • Learn to identify and exploit authorization bypass flaws
  • Practice parameter manipulation techniques
hints: Try changing the user_id parameter to access other users' data
Administrative users might have higher user ID numbers
Look for sensitive information in the API response

A02.title: Weak Encryption Analysis
A02.description:
This challenge demonstrates Cryptographic Failures through the use of weak encryption algorithms.

Scenario: The application uses a custom encryption service to protect sensitive data. However, the encryption algorithm implemented is fundamentally flawed and easily reversible.

Your Mission: Analyze the encryption algorithm, identify its weakness, and decrypt the protected data to reveal the flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Recognize weak encryption algorithms and their patterns
  • Understand the importance of using strong, proven cryptographic methods
  • Practice cryptanalysis techniques on vulnerable implementations
hints: The encryption algorithm used is ROT13 - a simple letter substitution cipher
ROT13 can be easily reversed by applying it again to the encrypted text
Try encrypting the word 'secret' to see if it reveals anything interesting

A03.title: SQL Injection Exploitation
A03.description:
This challenge demonstrates classic SQL Injection vulnerabilities in user input processing.

Scenario: The application has a user search feature that constructs SQL queries using unsanitized user input. The lack of proper input validation and parameterized queries creates an opportunity for SQL injection attacks.

Your Mission: Exploit the SQL injection vulnerability to extract sensitive information from the database, including hidden user records containing the flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand how SQL injection vulnerabilities occur
  • Practice constructing SQL injection payloads
  • Learn about UNION-based SQL injection techniques
hints: Try using single quotes to break out of the SQL query
Use UNION SELECT to combine results from multiple queries
The payload ' OR '1'='1' UNION SELECT * FROM users-- might be helpful

A04.title: Password Reset Design Flaw
A04.description:
This challenge demonstrates Insecure Design through a fundamentally flawed password reset mechanism.

Scenario: The application implements a password reset feature that lacks proper security controls. The design fails to implement verification mechanisms that should prevent unauthorized password changes.

Your Mission: Exploit the design flaw to reset an administrator's password without proper authorization and retrieve the flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Identify design-level security flaws vs implementation bugs
  • Understand the importance of threat modeling in secure design
  • Recognize missing security controls in application workflows
hints: The password reset doesn't require email verification or existing password
Try resetting the password for admin@company.com
No additional authentication is required to change someone else's password

A05.title: Debug Mode Information Disclosure
A05.description:
This challenge demonstrates Security Misconfiguration through exposed debug information.

Scenario: The application has debug mode functionality that was accidentally left enabled in production. When activated, this debug mode exposes sensitive configuration information that should never be visible to users.

Your Mission: Enable debug mode to access sensitive system information and locate the hidden flag in the exposed configuration data.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand the risks of leaving debug features enabled in production
  • Learn how misconfigurations can lead to information disclosure
  • Practice identifying and exploiting configuration vulnerabilities
hints: Look for ways to enable debug mode in the application
Debug information often contains sensitive configuration details
Try using the debug=true parameter

A06.title: Outdated Library Vulnerability
A06.description:
This challenge demonstrates Vulnerable and Outdated Components through the use of libraries with known security issues.

Scenario: The application uses outdated JavaScript libraries with known security vulnerabilities. A component version checker reveals information about the vulnerable versions in use.

Your Mission: Identify the outdated components and their associated vulnerabilities to locate the flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand the risks of using outdated software components
  • Learn to identify vulnerable library versions
  • Practice vulnerability research and CVE analysis
hints: Check the version information for popular JavaScript libraries
jQuery has had several known vulnerabilities in older versions
Use the component version checker to scan for outdated libraries

A07.title: Weak Password Brute Force
A07.description:
This challenge demonstrates Authentication Failures through weak password policies and inadequate brute force protection.

Scenario: The application implements a login system with weak password requirements and no brute force protection. Administrative accounts may be using commonly used weak passwords.

Your Mission: Exploit the weak authentication mechanism to gain access to an administrative account and retrieve the flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand the importance of strong password policies
  • Learn about common weak passwords and attack patterns
  • Practice credential-based attacks on weak authentication systems
hints: Try common weak passwords like '123456', 'password', 'admin'
The admin account might be using a predictable password
No account lockout protection is implemented

A08.title: Insecure Data Processing
A08.description:
This challenge demonstrates Software and Data Integrity Failures through unsafe data processing mechanisms.

Scenario: The application processes user-supplied data without proper integrity verification. This creates opportunities for attackers to manipulate the data processing workflow and potentially execute unauthorized operations.

Your Mission: Manipulate the data processing system to trigger the integrity failure and extract the hidden flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand data integrity verification requirements
  • Learn about unsafe deserialization vulnerabilities
  • Practice data manipulation techniques to exploit integrity failures
hints: The data processing system might execute special operations based on data content
Try including keywords like 'payload' and 'flag' in your data
Look for patterns that might trigger unsafe deserialization

A09.title: Audit Trail Bypass
A09.description:
This challenge demonstrates Security Logging and Monitoring Failures through inadequate audit trail implementation.

Scenario: The application performs various operations but fails to properly log security-sensitive actions. This lack of monitoring allows attackers to perform malicious activities without detection.

Your Mission: Perform a sensitive operation that should be logged but isn't, demonstrating the logging failure and obtaining the flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand the importance of comprehensive security logging
  • Learn to identify gaps in audit trail coverage
  • Practice exploiting insufficient monitoring controls
hints: Look for operations that seem security-sensitive
Try performing actions that should normally be logged
Sensitive operations might not be properly audited

A10.title: Internal Resource Access
A10.description:
This challenge demonstrates Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) through improper URL validation.

Scenario: The application provides a URL fetching service that retrieves content from user-specified URLs. However, it fails to properly validate and restrict the URLs that can be accessed, allowing attackers to make requests to internal resources.

Your Mission: Exploit the SSRF vulnerability to access internal services and retrieve sensitive information including the flag.

Learning Objectives:

  • Understand how SSRF vulnerabilities work and their impact
  • Learn to identify and exploit URL validation bypasses
  • Practice accessing internal services through SSRF
hints: Try accessing internal network addresses like localhost or 127.0.0.1
Internal services might expose sensitive information
The application doesn't properly validate the destination URLs
